questions.question: Which Indian city is to play host to the ‘MotoGP Bharat race’?
questions.explanation: The Buddh International Circuit (BIC) in Greater Noida is set to host India’s inaugural MotoGP Bharat race, making it the 31st country to host a motorcycle grand prix. Despite having previously held Formula 1 races, this will mark the first time India hosts the premier motorcycle race.

questions.question: Which country launched the ‘Cleanup to clean energy’ plan?
questions.explanation: As part of its ‘Cleanup to Clean energy’ plan, the US government has announced that it will transform Cold War sites into largest solar power project. Formerly employed for atomic bomb development, these sites are now positioned to significantly contribute to promoting clean energy initiatives throughout the United States.

questions.question: Typhoon Doksuri has hit which country recently?
questions.explanation: In the city of Beijing, the amount of rain over the past week has broken a 140-year-old record after being hit by Typhoon Doksuri. Typhoon Doksuri’s aftermath is causing widespread devastation, leading to thousands of evacuations. At least 10 people have died and 18 are missing, and officials have evacuated millions of people.

questions.question: Which country has organised ‘Operation Shield’ exercise?
questions.explanation: Brazil’s Operation Shield is a police operation that targeted criminal gangs in slums areas in Rio de Janeiro. At least 45 people have lost their lives in a recent series of lethal security force operations conducted in Brazil.

questions.question: As per recent data, what is the number of hours of housework per day done by women in India?
questions.explanation: As per a recent survey, women do seven hours of housework per day and men under three hours in India. The Tamil Nadu government launched camps to facilitate the registration of applicants for the Kalaignar Magalir Urimai Thogai Thittam, or women’s basic income scheme. The scheme will provide Rs. 1,000 per month to women in eligible households.

questions.question: Which country has proposed plans to limit the usage of smartphones by children?
questions.explanation: China’s cyberspace watchdog has proposed plans to limit the usage of smartphones by children to no more than two hours a day and to require all technology companies to introduce a ‘minor mode’ to enable restrictions. A draft “Guidelines for the Construction of Minor Mode of the Mobile Internet” is open for public comments. China is set to introduce some of the world’s most strict regulations for children in the usage of smartphones.

questions.question: Which country is the host of Durand Cup 2023?
questions.explanation: Asia’s oldest football tournament Durand Cup has commenced in India. A total of 43 matches will be played across four venues in Assam and West Bengal, which include Guwahati, Kokrajhar and Kolkata. The Durand Cup will feature 24 teams. The roster includes teams from the Indian Super League, I-League and the Armed Forces. Army teams from Nepal and Bangladesh have also been included this season.

questions.question: If a breach of privilege is believed to have committed by Members of Parliament, a motion will be referred to which committee?
questions.explanation: Parliamentary privilege grants specific rights to Members of Parliament, including freedom of expression during debates without the risk of court proceedings. If a breach of privilege is believed to have occurred, any member can raise a motion that will be referred to the Privileges Committee. Recently, Rajya Sabha Chairman Jagdeep Dhankhar has referred complaints concerning the privilege of the House against TMC’s Derek O’Brien and AAP’s Raghav Chadha to the privileges committee.

questions.question: Finance Ministry imposed anti-dumping duty on which product imports from China, South Korea and Indonesia?
questions.explanation: The Finance Ministry has imposed anti-dumping duty on certain optical fibre imports from China, South Korea and Indonesia, based on the recommendations of the Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR). It aims to give relief to the domestic optical fibre industry reeling under the adverse impact of low-price and low-quality imports.

questions.question: A recently discovered coronavirus variant named Eris, also known as EG.5.1, is rapidly spreading in which country?
questions.explanation: A recently discovered coronavirus variant named Eris, also known as EG.5.1, is rapidly spreading throughout the United Kingdom, causing concern among health authorities. This variant, which originated from the highly transmissible Omicron variant, was initially identified in the country last month.
